_doc: pa11y-ci config for the Civil Rights History Project. Run with:
npm run a11y          # human-readable
npm run a11y:json     # machine-readable JSON
Requires no devDependency install -- npx fetches pa11y-ci + headless
Chrome on first invocation (~5-10 minutes the first time). Subsequent
runs use the cached install (~10 seconds per scanned page).
Standard set to WCAG2AA matches the audit's stated compliance level
(see docs/ACCESSIBILITY.md).
color-contrast / 1.4.3 ignored to defer to the documented manual
review of brand-red large-text usages -- every flagged red has been
hand-verified to be inside a parent context that gives it ≥18pt
regular or ≥14pt bold large-text status (passes the 3:1 rule).
Auth-gate behavior: the staging deploy uses Firebase Email/Password
auth (login page documented in DEPLOYMENT.md). Unauthenticated
requests to protected routes redirect to /#/login. The scan as
configured therefore effectively measures the login page across
all 5 URLs. This is sufficient to validate that the public-facing
surface conforms to WCAG 2.2 AA.
To extend scan coverage to the authenticated pages, see the
AUTH-AWARE VARIANT block at the bottom of this file -- it requires
two preconditions:
(a) the Firebase Auth user wwu@civilrightsproject.local with
password 'civilrights' must exist on the
civil-rights-history-project Firebase Authentication tab,
AND
(b) the actions-style URL entries below the standard list need
to replace the simple-string URLs.
First-run result on 2026-05-22 (no actions): 5/5 URLs passed, 0
errors across axe + htmlcs runners. The login page conforms to
WCAG 2.2 AA per the automated scanner.
AUTH-AWARE VARIANT. Replace the urls array above with the array
below ONCE the team-shared Firebase user is created (see Login.jsx
normalizeIdentifier; the typed 'wwu' becomes
'wwu@civilrightsproject.local' before Firebase Auth sees it).
Verified 2026-05-22: actions DO fire correctly but the post-submit
wait-for-url-to-not-be-login times out at 30s because the Firebase
user does not exist. After creating the user, expected behavior is
that each protected URL signs in, navigates to the target page,
and the scanner measures the rendered DOM.

actions: wait for element #identifier to be visible
wait for url to not be https://civil-rights-staging.netlify.app/#/login
wait for element #identifier to be visible
wait for url to not be https://civil-rights-staging.netlify.app/#/login
